---
title: "Comparative Diagnostic Effectiveness Study of NDT Imaging Modalities for Internal-Defect Detection"
subtitle: "Study design"
author:
  - name: "Clarinet Demo Team"
date: today
date-format: "D MMMM YYYY"
lang: en

format:
  docx:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    toc-title: "Contents"
    number-sections: true
    number-depth: 3
    highlight-style: github
    fig-width: 10
    fig-height: 14
---

## Objective

Evaluate the diagnostic effectiveness of different non-destructive testing (NDT) imaging modalities in detecting internal defects.

## Participants

1. **Lead Inspector (monitor)** — 1 CT/UT inspector with at least 5 years' NDT experience
2. **Group A** — inspectors with at least 5 years' NDT experience
3. **Group B** — 2 inspectors with at least 1 year of CT-HD viewing experience
4. **Group C** — repair technicians with at least 5 years' experience
5. **Expert** — specialist who creates and updates the reference master model

## Modalities studied

When a part meets the inclusion criteria, it is scheduled for a repair operation. Shortly before the repair operation, the following scans are performed on it:
- **CT** — industrial computed tomography
- **UT** — ultrasonic volumetric scan
- **CT-HD** — high-resolution/enhanced CT
- **UT-HD** — high-resolution UT
- **MCT** — micro-CT

Additionally:
- **Baseline archive** — prior CT scans of the same part, taken before an earlier repair/rework cycle (there may be several such studies)

Each modality is represented by **one** study (except the baseline archive). Segmentation is not performed directly on the baseline archive. However, CT segmentation is done in two variants: as an isolated series, and together with the baseline archive.

## Core concept: master model

The **master model** is a reference segmentation of all defects detected in a part, where each defect occupies a separate region of interest (ROI) with a unique number.

The master model's basis is the part's first CT with a completed segmentation. The coordinate grid (pixel count, pixel spacing, coordinate origin) is defined by the reference series from the initial quality assessment and remains unchanged.

The master model is created once and is extended with new defects as they're found on studies from other modalities. Each new defect gets a unique number.

For each modality (UT, CT-HD, UT-HD, MCT), a **projection** of the master model is created — transferring defects from the CT coordinate space into that modality's coordinate space.

## Study stages

### 1. Quality assessment (manual)

Performed by Group A inspectors (2 independent assessments).

This stage determines:
- Whether the study is suitable for further work
- Modality type (CT, UT, CT-HD, UT-HD, MCT, baseline archive)
- **Reference series** — the series with the fewest artifacts and the most reference landmarks, on which defects will be marked

Defects are searched across all available series, but marking is only done on the reference series.

### 2. Anonymization (automatic)

Runs automatically once the study passes quality assessment. All part-identifying data is replaced with anonymous identifiers.

### 3. Defect segmentation (manual)

**Who performs it:** inspectors with the corresponding modality specialization

- CT → inspectors specializing in CT
- UT → inspectors specializing in UT
- CT-HD → inspectors specializing in CT-HD
- UT-HD → inspectors specializing in UT (no separate UT-HD specialization role exists)
- MCT → inspectors specializing in MCT

Each study is marked by two inspectors independently.

**Available information:**

- CT (standard context): only the current study
- CT (extended context): current study + all baseline archive studies for the part
- Other modalities: only the current study

**Process:**

1. The inspector views the anonymized study in the OHIF viewer
2. Loads the reference series in 3D Slicer
3. Marks defects in three categories: defect, indeterminate, cosmetic

A feature of this stage is that assessment of CT with archive data always happens after the assessment of the isolated study has been completed. If an inspector is assigned to assess several modalities, the modalities are presented in order from the potentially least sensitive method to the most sensitive: CT, UT, CT-HD, UT-HD, MCT, CT with archive. At least 2 days must pass between one inspector's assessments of different modalities, or the inspector must have assessed at least 10 other studies in between.

Segmentation is performed on the reference series, but the inspector may view all available series.

### 4. Initial master model construction

Performed by the expert after the first completed CT segmentation with archive data (from any inspector). The coordinate grid for the model is defined by the CT reference series. Each defect in the segmentation gets a separate label matching its unique number.

### 5. Master model projection (manual)

Performed by the expert for each new modality (UT, CT-HD, UT-HD, MCT).

In 3D Slicer, the expert views the reference series of the target modality and the CT with the master model overlaid. Each ROI from the master model is placed, in turn, into the target modality series' coordinate space. If a defect isn't visible on the target modality, the ROI is placed based on anatomical landmarks. Locating a defect on the target modality study isn't limited to the reference series — other series of that study can also be viewed.

On save, the projection is validated: its segment names must exactly match the master model's segment names (i.e. every defect from the master model is represented, and no extra ones are added).

### 6. Comparing projection and segmentation (automatic)

Matching each modality's series segmentation to the master model is done via projections. An automatic per-ROI comparison of segmentation and projection is performed: for each ROI, the pipeline checks for overlap — overlap present means the same defect; no overlap means a false negative or false positive.

Defects can thus be classified as: detected, missed, or additionally detected. Additionally detected defects are ones found during segmentation but absent from the master model.

When additionally detected defects are present, the study moves to the master model update stage.

When missed defects are present, the inspector who performed the segmentation moves to the "Second review" stage.

This stage runs for each segmentation independently (if 2 inspectors produced 2 segmentations, 2 comparisons are run).

### 7. Second review

Performed by the inspector whose segmentation diverges from the master model projection. The inspector gets the reference series image in 3D Slicer with the defects from the projection that they missed (each under its own number). Their task is to assign each defect to one of four categories: defect, indeterminate, cosmetic, invisible.

This task is always performed by the inspector whose segmentation showed the discrepancies. The inspector doesn't know which modality the defect was found on or how it was characterized — they only see the region of interest on the master model projection. If the master model update reveals new discrepancies, the task is invalidated and must be redone, but now for the new defects. All results of this stage are stored in a single file regardless of the number of iterations.

On save, the inspector only has access to classification of defects from the master model projection — marking new defects is not available.

### 8. Master model update

When the projection-vs-segmentation comparison stage finds additionally detected defects, the expert is given a master model update task.

In 3D Slicer, the expert is presented with the master model with the CT reference series, the projection of the model onto the modality, and the additionally detected defects overlaid on the projection (highlighted in a separate color). The expert must transfer these additional defects from the projection onto the master model.

Numbering of defects added to the master model is assigned automatically. To avoid race conditions and duplicated work transferring a newly found defect, only one update task can be active at any given time, even if additional defects were found on different modalities.

After the master model update, the projection and comparison stages are invalidated and redone.
New tasks are created for experts to edit projections, and for inspectors.

The master model's coordinate grid does not change.

Updates never remove existing defects, only add new ones. Defect numbers on the master model are immutable — on save, it's validated that defects previously present on the master model have not changed number.

**Problem:** the master model can be updated while a projection is being created. As a result, the projection would be saved after the master model save.

**Solution:**
- When a projection is created, a checksum (hash) of the master model used is stored
- On projection completion, the hash is compared against the current master model file
- On mismatch, the projection is invalidated and a new task is created
- No work is lost — the check only happens at task completion

## Example sequence

1. **CT:** initial quality assessment → reference series determination → inspector segmentation → master model creation → projection onto the same series (a copy)
2. **UT:** initial quality assessment → reference series determination → inspector segmentation → expert creates the master model projection → automatic comparison
3. **Discrepancy:** 1 defect found on the UT segmentation, absent from the projection (false positive) → a master model update task is created
4. **Update:** the expert adds the new defect to the master model → all existing projections are invalidated
5. **Repeat projections:** experts re-mark projections for all modalities
6. **Second review:** review tasks are created for inspectors whose segmentations diverged from the updated projection
7. **CT-HD:** initial assessment → segmentation → projection → comparison → no discrepancies found
8. **MCT:** same cycle → no discrepancies found

## Workflow diagram

```{mermaid}
%%| label: fig-workflow
%%| fig-cap: "Full workflow diagram of the NDT comparative defect-detection study"
%%| fig-width: 10
%%| fig-height: 14

flowchart TD
    %% Start of process
    Start([Part: 5 modalities + baseline archive]) --> QA1[Quality assessment]

    %% Quality assessment
    QA1 -->|2 independent assessments| QA2{Suitable?}
    QA2 -->|No| Reject[Study rejected]
    QA2 -->|Yes| QA3[Reference series determination]

    %% Anonymization
    QA3 --> Anon[Anonymization — automatic]

    %% Split by modality
    Anon --> Split{Modality routing}

    %% CT (isolated)
    Split -->|CT isolated| CT1[CT segmentation]
    CT1 -->|2 inspectors independently| CT1_Done

    %% CT with archive — builds the master model
    Split -->|CT + archive| CT2[CT + archive segmentation]
    CT2 -->|2 inspectors independently| CT2_Done[CT+archive complete]
    CT2_Done -->|First completed| MM_Create[Expert: master model creation]

    %% Other modalities
    Split -->|UT| UT[UT segmentation]
    Split -->|CT-HD| CTHD[CT-HD segmentation]
    Split -->|UT-HD| UTHD[UT-HD segmentation]
    Split -->|MCT| MCT[MCT segmentation]

    UT -->|2 inspectors independently| UT_Done
    CTHD -->|2 inspectors independently| CTHD_Done
    UTHD -->|2 inspectors independently| UTHD_Done
    MCT -->|2 inspectors independently| MCT_Done

    %% Create projections for all modalities
    MM_Create --> MM_Ready[Master model ready]

    MM_Ready --> Proj_CT1[Projection for isolated CT]
    MM_Ready --> Proj_UT[Projection for UT]
    MM_Ready --> Proj_CTHD[Projection for CT-HD]
    MM_Ready --> Proj_UTHD[Projection for UT-HD]
    MM_Ready --> Proj_MCT[Projection for MCT]

    %% Compare projections with segmentations
    CT1_Done --> Comp_CT1[Automatic comparison]
    Proj_CT1 --> Comp_CT1

    UT_Done --> Comp_UT[Automatic comparison]
    Proj_UT --> Comp_UT

    CTHD_Done --> Comp_CTHD[Automatic comparison]
    Proj_CTHD --> Comp_CTHD

    UTHD_Done --> Comp_UTHD[Automatic comparison]
    Proj_UTHD --> Comp_UTHD

    MCT_Done --> Comp_MCT[Automatic comparison]
    Proj_MCT --> Comp_MCT

    %% Analyze comparison results
    Comp_CT1 --> Analysis_CT1{CT result}
    Comp_UT --> Analysis_UT{UT result}
    Comp_CTHD --> Analysis_CTHD{CT-HD result}
    Comp_UTHD --> Analysis_UTHD{UT-HD result}
    Comp_MCT --> Analysis_MCT{MCT result}

    %% Handle additional defects
    Analysis_CT1 -->|Additional defects| Update_MM
    Analysis_UT -->|Additional defects| Update_MM
    Analysis_CTHD -->|Additional defects| Update_MM
    Analysis_UTHD -->|Additional defects| Update_MM
    Analysis_MCT -->|Additional defects| Update_MM

    Update_MM[Expert: master model update]
    Update_MM -->|Invalidate all projections| MM_Ready

    %% Handle missed defects
    Analysis_CT1 -->|Missed defects| Review_CT1[CT review]
    Analysis_UT -->|Missed defects| Review_UT[UT review]
    Analysis_CTHD -->|Missed defects| Review_CTHD[CT-HD review]
    Analysis_UTHD -->|Missed defects| Review_UTHD[UT-HD review]
    Analysis_MCT -->|Missed defects| Review_MCT[MCT review]

    Review_CT1 --> Review_CT1_Done[Defect classification]
    Review_UT --> Review_UT_Done[Defect classification]
    Review_CTHD --> Review_CTHD_Done[Defect classification]
    Review_UTHD --> Review_UTHD_Done[Defect classification]
    Review_MCT --> Review_MCT_Done[Defect classification]

    %% Completion
    Analysis_CT1 -->|No discrepancies| Done_CT1[CT complete]
    Analysis_UT -->|No discrepancies| Done_UT[UT complete]
    Analysis_CTHD -->|No discrepancies| Done_CTHD[CT-HD complete]
    Analysis_UTHD -->|No discrepancies| Done_UTHD[UT-HD complete]
    Analysis_MCT -->|No discrepancies| Done_MCT[MCT complete]

    Review_CT1_Done --> Done_CT1
    Review_UT_Done --> Done_UT
    Review_CTHD_Done --> Done_CTHD
    Review_UTHD_Done --> Done_UTHD
    Review_MCT_Done --> Done_MCT

    Done_CT1 --> Final{All modalities complete?}
    Done_UT --> Final
    Done_CTHD --> Final
    Done_UTHD --> Final
    Done_MCT --> Final

    Final -->|Yes| Complete([Study complete])
    Final -->|No| Wait[Waiting for other modalities]

    %% Styles
    classDef automatic fill:#e1f5ff,stroke:#0066cc,stroke-width:2px
    classDef manual fill:#fff4e1,stroke:#ff9900,stroke-width:2px
    classDef expert fill:#ffe1f5,stroke:#cc0066,stroke-width:2px
    classDef decision fill:#f0f0f0,stroke:#666,stroke-width:2px

    class Anon,Comp_CT1,Comp_UT,Comp_CTHD,Comp_UTHD,Comp_MCT automatic
    class QA1,QA3,CT1,CT2,UT,CTHD,UTHD,MCT,Review_CT1,Review_UT,Review_CTHD,Review_UTHD,Review_MCT manual
    class MM_Create,Proj_CT1,Proj_UT,Proj_CTHD,Proj_UTHD,Proj_MCT,Update_MM expert
    class QA2,Split,Analysis_CT1,Analysis_UT,Analysis_CTHD,Analysis_UTHD,Analysis_MCT,Final decision
```

### Legend

- **Blue** (light blue fill) — automatic processes
- **Orange** (yellow fill) — manual processes (inspectors)
- **Pink** — expert tasks
- **Gray** — decision points

### Key workflow features

1. **Parallel modality processing**: all 5 modalities (CT, UT, CT-HD, UT-HD, MCT) are processed independently

2. **Update loops**: when additional defects are found, the master model is updated and all projections are invalidated

3. **Double independent assessment**: each segmentation is performed by two inspectors independently

4. **Second review**: inspectors review missed defects after comparison against the projection
